identificationModule.briefTitle: A Study to Find a Suitable Dose of ASP2802 in People With CD20-positive B-cell Lymphomas
identificationModule.officialTitle: A First-in-Human, Phase 1 Study to Assess the Safety, Pharmacokinetics, Pharmacodynamics and Preliminary Efficacy of a Single-Induction Dose of convertibleCARTM-T Cells Armed With MicAbodyTM Protein (ASP2802) Followed by Maintenance Booster Doses of the MicAbody Protein in Patients With CD20-Positive Relapsed or Refractory B-Cell Lymphomas

statusModule.whyStopped: The termination of the study was a business decision made by the sponsor.

descriptionModule.briefSummary: ASP2802 is a type of CAR-T cell therapy given with MA-20. This adaptive study evaluates a single-induction CAR-T dose followed by maintenance booster doses of MA-20 for relapsed or refractory CD20-positive B-cell lymphomas.

armGroups.label: Group A: ASP2802 Low Dose followed by MA-20
armGroups.description: Participants receive ASP2802 on Day 0 and then maintenance booster doses of MA-20 in each 28-day cycle.

armGroups.label: Group B: ASP2802 Intermediate Dose followed by MA-20
armGroups.description: Participants receive an intermediate ASP2802 dose followed by MA-20 booster dosing.

armGroups.label: Group C: ASP2802 High Dose followed by MA-20
armGroups.description: Participants receive a higher ASP2802 dose followed by MA-20 booster dosing.

primaryOutcomes.description: DLTs are assessed after ASP2802 infusion and subsequent MA-20 doses during dose escalation.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Adults with histologically confirmed CD20-positive relapsed or refractory B-cell lymphoma after at least 2 prior lines of therapy are eligible.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Low Dose Radiation as Bridging Therapy in Relapsed B-Cell Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma
identificationModule.officialTitle: Feasibility of Low Dose Radiation as Bridging Therapy for Lisocabtagene Maraleucel in Relapsed B-Cell Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This trial evaluates whether bridging radiation before CAR T-cell infusion is feasible and safe in relapsed B-cell lymphoma.

conditions: DLBCL - Diffuse Large B Cell Lymphoma

armGroups.label: Single arm
armGroups.description: Subjects receive bridging radiation following pheresis and prior to lymphodepleting chemotherapy and CAR T-cell infusion.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Feasibility of the intervention in the proposed study population
primaryOutcomes.description: Percentage of subjects who proceed to CAR T-cell infusion after bridging radiation.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Adults with relapsed or progressive B-cell lymphoma who meet FDA-approved criteria for liso-cel infusion and have at least one site safe for radiation are eligible.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Descartes-11 Consolidation Treatment in Patients With High-Risk Multiple Myeloma Who Have Residual Disease After Induction Therapy
identificationModule.officialTitle: Descartes-11 Consolidation Treatment in Patients With High-Risk Multiple Myeloma Who Have Residual Disease After Induction Therapy

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This study assesses MRD-negative complete response after consolidation treatment with Descartes-11 in high-risk myeloma after induction therapy.

armGroups.label: Descartes 11
armGroups.description: CAR T-cell consolidation after induction therapy.

primaryOutcomes.description: Stringent complete response is defined according to IMWG response criteria.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Adults with high-risk multiple myeloma and residual disease after induction therapy are eligible.
